notes: Bundled knowledge base for authorized Crosshair workflows. Add more JSON files in this folder to extend the app later.

phases.name: Reconnaissance

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.summary: Actively probe in-scope infrastructure to identify hosts, services, and exposed applications.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Network Information
techniques.summary: Collect authorized network ranges, names, routing, and externally visible infrastructure.

techniques.name: Search Open Technical Databases
techniques.summary: Review approved open-source technical data such as certificates, DNS, and service banners.

defensiveUse: Confirm owned address space before scans.
Capture timestamped evidence for every recon action.
Map each exposed service to an owner, business purpose, and validation status.

phases.name: Discovery

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.summary: Enumerate ports and services on authorized hosts.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.summary: Identify reachable systems and relationships between network segments.

defensiveUse: Compare discovered services against the expected inventory.
Flag externally reachable administrative services for validation.

phases.name: Vulnerability Validation

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.summary: Use controlled checks to confirm whether a public-facing finding is actually exploitable.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.summary: Validate exposed remote access paths through approved, non-destructive tests.

defensiveUse: Prefer proof through version, configuration, and safe test evidence before exploit simulation.

phases.name: Remediation Retest

techniques.name: Patch Verification
techniques.summary: Repeat the original safe check after remediation and store closure evidence.

defensiveUse: Retest with the same scoped target and tool profile.
Record before and after evidence in Postgres.
